devanagari: जरासन्धरुद्धानां राज्ञां कारागृहान् मोचनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । अयुते द्वे शतान्यष्टौ निरुद्धा युधि निर्जिताः । ते निर्गता गिरिद्रोण्यां मल...
english_devnagari: jarāsandharuddhānāṃ rājñāṃ kārāgṛhān mocanam śrīśuka uvāca ayute dve śatānyaṣṭau niruddhā yudhi nirjitāḥ te nirgatā giridroṇyāṃ malinā malavāsasaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Twenty thousand eight hundred kings who were easily defeated in battle (by Jarāsandha) were kept confined in a valley surrounded on all sides by the mountain. (Released from captivity) they came out with unclean bodies and dirty clothes.

devanagari: क्षुत्क्षामाः शुष्कवदनाः संरोधपरिकर्शिताः । ददृशुस्ते घनश्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣutkṣāmāḥ śuṣkavadanāḥ saṃrodhaparikarśitāḥ dadṛśuste ghanaśyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasam

translation: Emaciated with hunger and with mouths parched up and greatly reduced in bodies due to confinement for a long time, the kings saw Lord Kṛṣṇa of dark blue complexion like a cloud, wearing yellow silken garments.

devanagari: श्रीवत्साङ्कं चतुर्बाहुं पद्मगर्भारुणेक्षणम् । चारुप्रसन्नवदनं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvatsāṅkaṃ caturbāhuṃ padmagarbhāruṇekṣaṇam cāruprasannavadanaṃ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalam

[nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]

translation: He was bearing the mark of Śrīvatsa; had four arms. His eyes were reddish like the inner part of a lotus-flower and he was charming and gracious in appearance. He was adorned with brilliant alligator-shaped ear-rings.

devanagari: पद्महस्तं गदाशङ्ख रथाङ्गैरुपलक्षितम् । किरीटहारकटक कटिसूत्राङ्गदाञ्चितम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: padmahastaṃ gadāśaṅkha rathāṅgairupalakṣitam kirīṭahārakaṭaka kaṭisūtrāṅgadāñcitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ añc ], [accusative single from √ añc ]

translation: He was holding a lotus in one of his hands and was wielding a mace, a conch and a discus (Sudarśana) in the remaining three arms. He was adorned with a crown, a necklace of pearls, bracelets, a zone and a pair of armlets.

devanagari: भ्राजद्वरमणिग्रीवं निवीतं वनमालया । पिबन्त इव चक्षुर्भ्यां लिहन्त इव जिह्वया ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrājadvaramaṇigrīvaṃ nivītaṃ vanamālayā pibanta iva cakṣurbhyāṃ lihanta iva jihvayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pā class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ lih class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ lih class 2 verb]

translation: His neck was shining with the excellent jewel Kaustubha and had a garland of forest-flowers (Vanamālā) hanging on his bosom. The kings appeared to drink him with eyes and to lick him with tongues.

devanagari: जिघ्रन्त इव नासाभ्यां रम्भन्त इव बाहुभिः । प्रणेमुर्हतपाप्मानो मूर्धभिः पादयोर्हरेः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: jighranta iva nāsābhyāṃ rambhanta iva bāhubhiḥ praṇemurhatapāpmāno mūrdhabhiḥ pādayorhareḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ rambh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ rambh class 1 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: They were as if smelling him with their noses and embracing him with their arms. They bowed to the Lord with their heads touching his feet and rid themselves of all sins.

devanagari: कृष्णसन्दर्शनाह्लाद ध्वस्तसंरोधनक्लमाः । प्रशशंसुर्हृषीकेशं गीर्भिः प्राञ्जलयो नृपाः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasandarśanāhlāda dhvastasaṃrodhanaklamāḥ praśaśaṃsurhṛṣīkeśaṃ gīrbhiḥ prāñjalayo nṛpāḥ
translation: At the sight of Śrī Kṛṣṇa they were so much overjoyed that they got over the languor and exhaustion caused by their incarceration. Folding their palms, the kings eulogized Hṛṣīkeśa (Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of all senses) in the following words:

devanagari: राजान ऊचुः । नमस्ते देवदेवेश प्रपन्नार्तिहराव्यय । प्रपन्ना पाहि नः कृष्ण निर्विण्णान् घोरसंसृतेः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājāna ūcuḥ namaste devadeveśa prapannārtiharāvyaya prapannā pāhi naḥ kṛṣṇa nirviṇṇān ghorasaṃsṛteḥ
translation:
The kings submitted their prayer:
We bow to you, O God of Gods. You remove the distress of those that seek shelter in you, O Immutable Lord, Being disgusted with the terrible saṃsāra we have resorted to you. Pray, be pleased to protect us, O Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: नैनं नाथानुसूयामो मागधं मधुसूदन । अनुग्रहो यद् भवतो राज्ञां राज्यच्युतिर्विभो ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nainaṃ nāthānusūyāmo māgadhaṃ madhusūdana anugraho yad bhavato rājñāṃ rājyacyutirvibho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O Lord! Slayer of the demon Madhu! We do not blame that Jarāsandha, king of Magadhas. It is really your Grace that we kings were deprived of our kingdoms, O Almighty Lord.

devanagari: राज्यैश्वर्यमदोन्नद्धो न श्रेयो विन्दते नृपः । त्वन्मायामोहितोऽनित्या मन्यते सम्पदोऽचलाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: rājyaiśvaryamadonnaddho na śreyo vindate nṛpaḥ tvanmāyāmohito

translation: A king elated with pride of sovereignty and affluence does not attain the Final Beatitude, but being infatuated by your deluding potency (Māyā) looks upon the transient worldly fortune as permanent.

devanagari: मृगतृष्णां यथा बाला मन्यन्त उदकाशयम् । एवं वैकारिकीं मायां अयुक्ता वस्तु चक्षते ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛgatṛṣṇāṃ yathā bālā manyanta udakāśayam evaṃ vaikārikīṃ māyāṃ ayuktā vastu cakṣate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third plural], [present passive third plural]

translation: Just as children (or foolish people) mistake a mirage as a pool of water, persons without discriminatory power, ascribe reality to the everchanging Māyā which brings about modifications.

devanagari: वयं पुरा श्रीमदनष्टदृष्टयो । जिगीषयास्या इतरेतरस्पृधः । घ्नन्तः प्रजाः स्वा अतिनिर्घृणाः प्रभो । मृत्युं पुरस्त्वाविगणय्य दुर्मदाः ॥ १...
english_devnagari: vayaṃ purā śrīmadanaṣṭadṛṣṭayo jigīṣayāsyā itaretaraspṛdhaḥ ghnantaḥ prajāḥ svā atinirghṛṇāḥ prabho mṛtyuṃ purastvāvigaṇayya durmadāḥ

translation: O Lord! Formerly we were blinded by our pride of kingship and affluence. In our ambition to conquer this earth, we competed with each other slaughtering mercilessly our own subjects. We were so madly proud that we did not think of death or of yourself, who were ahead of us.

devanagari: त एव कृष्णाद्य गभीररंहसा । दुरन्तवीर्येण विचालिताः श्रियः । कालेन तन्वा भवतोऽनुकम्पया । विनष्टदर्पाश्चरणौ स्मराम ते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ta eva kṛṣṇādya gabhīraraṃhasā durantavīryeṇa vicālitāḥ śriyaḥ kālena tanvā bhavato vinaṣṭadarpāścaraṇau smarāma te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ cal ], [vocative plural from √ cal ]
[nominative plural from √ cal ], [vocative plural from √ cal ], [accusative plural from √ cal ]

translation: O Kṛṣṇa! By your form called Time, of inexorable velocity and infinite prowess, we were despoiled of our former affluence and sovereignty. It is through your Grace that today we, with our pride completely destroyed, are contemplating on your feet.

devanagari: अथो न राज्यम्मृगतृष्णिरूपितं । देहेन शश्वत् पतता रुजां भुवा । उपासितव्यं स्पृहयामहे विभो । क्रियाफलं प्रेत्य च कर्णरोचनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: atho na rājyammṛgatṛṣṇirūpitaṃ dehena śaśvat patatā rujāṃ bhuvā upāsitavyaṃ spṛhayāmahe vibho kriyāphalaṃ pretya ca karṇarocanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rūp class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ rūp class 10 verb]

translation: We do not aspire after this mirage like sovereignty which is to be enjoyed by this ever-decaying body, a nursery of ailments). Nor do we seek to enjoy after death the fruits of our (religious or meritorious) actions (in heaven) which sound so pleasant to the ears, O Almighty Lord.

devanagari: तं नः समादिशोपायं येन ते चरणाब्जयोः । स्मृतिर्यथा न विरमेद् अपि संसरतामिह ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ naḥ samādiśopāyaṃ yena te caraṇābjayoḥ smṛtiryathā na viramed api saṃsaratāmiha
translation: Pray, be gracious to teach us, that expedient whereby we shall never lose the memory of your lotus-like feet (and continue to contemplate on them) even while we are involved in the series of births and deaths.

devanagari: कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय हरये परमात्मने । प्रणतक्लेशनाशाय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇāya vāsudevāya haraye paramātmane praṇatakleśanāśāya govindāya namo namaḥ
translation: We repeatedly pay our salutations to Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Vasudeva, the destroyer of the misery of saṃsāra (even of Daityas), the Supreme Self. You are the remover of sufferings of those who bow to you, O Govinda (Lord of Senses).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । संस्तूयमानो भगवान् राजभिर्मुक्तबन्धनैः । तानाह करुणस्तात शरण्यः श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca saṃstūyamāno bhagavān rājabhirmuktabandhanaiḥ tānāha karuṇastāta śaraṇyaḥ ślakṣṇayā girā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
O dear Parīkṣit! Thus eulogised by the kings who were now freed from captivity, the merciful Lord who affords protection to those who seek it, spoke to them in sweet language as follows:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अद्य प्रभृति वो भूपा मय्यात्मन्यखिलेश्वरे । सुदृढा जायते भक्तिः बाढमाशंसितं तथा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca adya prabhṛti vo bhūpā mayyātmanyakhileśvare sudṛḍhā jāyate bhaktiḥ bāḍhamāśaṃsitaṃ tathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
O monarchs! As desired by you, from now onwards your unflinching devotion unto me who am the Ruler of the universe and the Self, abiding in all, shall grow strong and deep-rooted.

devanagari: दिष्ट्या व्यवसितं भूपा भवन्त ऋतभाषिणः । श्रीयैश्वर्यमदोन्नाहं पश्य उन्मादकं नृणाम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā vyavasitaṃ bhūpā bhavanta ṛtabhāṣiṇaḥ śrīyaiśvaryamadonnāhaṃ paśya unmādakaṃ nṛṇām
translation: How glad I am at your correct resolution, O kings. What you expressed is the truth. For I have seen how growing pride of affluence and authority has a maddening effect on people.

devanagari: हैहयो नहुषो वेनो रावणो नरकोऽपरे । श्रीमदाद् भ्रंशिताः स्थानाद् देवदैत्यनरेश्वराः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: haihayo nahuṣo veno rāvaṇo narako śrīmadād bhraṃśitāḥ sthānād devadaityanareśvarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ bhraṃś ], [vocative plural from √ bhraṃś ]
[nominative plural from √ bhraṃś ], [vocative plural from √ bhraṃś ], [accusative plural from √ bhraṃś ]

translation: Haihaya (Sahasrārjuna), Nahuṣa, Vena, Rāvaṇa, Naraka and other rulers of gods, demons and human beings have fallen down from their positions, in consequence of their arrogance, begotten of their prosperity and power.

devanagari: भवन्त एतद् विज्ञाय देहाद्युत्पाद्यमन्तवत् । मां यजन्तोऽध्वरैर्युक्ताः प्रजा धर्मेण रक्षथ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavanta etad vijñāya dehādyutpādyamantavat māṃ yajanto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ pad ]

[accusative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pad ]

translation: Being thoroughly cognisant of the fact that everything that is created like the body (and other things connected therewith) is perishable, you worship me by performing sacrifices and be alert and protect your subjects in a righteous manner, O monarchs.

devanagari: संतन्वन्तः प्रजातन्तून् सुखं दुःखं भवाभवौ । प्राप्तं प्राप्तं च सेवन्तो मच्चित्ता विचरिष्यथ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃtanvantaḥ prajātantūn sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ bhavābhavau prāptaṃ prāptaṃ ca sevanto maccittā vicariṣyatha
translation: You secure the continuity of your family by begetting children. Bear up with equable mind, the weal and woe, prosperity and adversity, birth or death as it comes to your lot and carry on your life devoting your heart to me.

devanagari: उदासीनाश्च देहादौ आत्मारामा धृतव्रताः । मय्यावेश्य मनः सम्यङ् मां अन्ते ब्रह्म यास्यथ ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: udāsīnāśca dehādau ātmārāmā dhṛtavratāḥ mayyāveśya manaḥ samyaṅ māṃ ante brahma yāsyatha

[vocative single from √ viś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ viś ]
[vocative single from √ viś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ viś ]

translation: Remain indifferent to your body and things connected with it. Take delight in the spiritual bliss of the self and observe vows of self-discipline. Thus, concentrating your mind properly and steadily in me, you will ultimately attain to me who am the supreme Brahman.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यादिश्य नृपान् कृष्णो भगवान् भुवनेश्वरः । तेषां न्ययुङ्क्त पुरुषान् स्त्रियो मज्जनकर्मणि ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityādiśya nṛpān kṛṣṇo bhagavān bhuvaneśvaraḥ teṣāṃ nyayuṅkta puruṣān striyo majjanakarmaṇi

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having enjoined the kings in the above manner. Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Ruler of the Universe ordered male and female servants to give them a bath.

devanagari: सपर्यां कारयामास सहदेवेन भारत । नरदेवोचितैर्वस्त्रैः भूषणैः स्रग्विलेपनैः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: saparyāṃ kārayāmāsa sahadevena bhārata naradevocitairvastraiḥ bhūṣaṇaiḥ sragvilepanaiḥ
translation: He made Sahadeva (now the king of Magadha) to receive them respectfully, by giving them clothes, ornaments, wreaths of flowers and pigments worthy of kings.

devanagari: भोजयित्वा वरान्नेन सुस्नातान् समलङ्कृतान् । भोगैश्च विविधैर्युक्तान् तांबूलाद्यैर्नृपोचितैः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhojayitvā varānnena susnātān samalaṅkṛtān bhogaiśca vividhairyuktān tāṃbūlādyairnṛpocitaiḥ
translation: When the kings had taken an excellent bath and put on their rich clothes and ornaments, they were served with dainty dishes and were offered various luxuries worthy of kings such as betel-leaves, etc.

devanagari: ते पूजिता मुकुन्देन राजानो मृष्टकुण्डलाः । विरेजुर्मोचिताः क्लेशात् प्रावृडन्ते यथा ग्रहाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: te pūjitā mukundena rājāno mṛṣṭakuṇḍalāḥ virejurmocitāḥ kleśāt prāvṛḍante yathā grahāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

[nominative plural from √ muc ], [vocative plural from √ muc ], [accusative plural from √ muc ]
[nominative plural from √ muc ], [vocative plural from √ muc ]
[nominative plural from √ muc ], [vocative plural from √ muc ], [accusative plural from √ muc ]

translation: Having been honoured by Mukunda, these kings, wearing brilliant ear-rings and freed from their distressful conditions, looked resplendent like planets at the end of the rainy season.

devanagari: रथान् सदश्वान् आरोप्य मणिकाञ्चनभूषितान् । प्रीणय्य सुनृतैर्वाक्यैः स्वदेशान् प्रत्ययापयत् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: rathān sadaśvān āropya maṇikāñcanabhūṣitān prīṇayya sunṛtairvākyaiḥ svadeśān pratyayāpayat
translation: Mounting them in chariots decorated with gold and jewels and drawn by good horses, and cheering up their hearts with sweet yet sincere words, they were sent to their respective countries.

devanagari: त एवं मोचिताः कृच्छ्रात् कृष्णेन सुमहात्मना । ययुस्तमेव ध्यायन्तः कृतानि च जगत्पतेः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ta evaṃ mocitāḥ kṛcchrāt kṛṣṇena sumahātmanā yayustameva dhyāyantaḥ kṛtāni ca jagatpateḥ

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: The kings who were thus relieved of their hardships by the highly noble-souled Kṛṣṇa, returned contemplating on Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the universe and his noble deeds.

devanagari: जगदुः प्रकृतिभ्यस्ते महापुरुषचेष्टितम् । यथान्वशासद् भगवान् तथा चक्रुरतन्द्रिताः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: jagaduḥ prakṛtibhyaste mahāpuruṣaceṣṭitam yathānvaśāsad bhagavān tathā cakruratandritāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation: (Arriving at their capitals) they told to their ministers (and subjects) the glorious deeds of that Supreme Person and carried on their life (and government) deligently [diligently?] as per instructions of the Lord.

devanagari: जरासन्धं घातयित्वा भीमसेनेन केशवः । पार्थाभ्यां संयुतः प्रायात् सहदेवेन पूजितः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: jarāsandhaṃ ghātayitvā bhīmasenena keśavaḥ pārthābhyāṃ saṃyutaḥ prāyāt sahadevena pūjitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: Having slain Jarāsandha through the instrumentation of Bhīma, Keśava was worshipped by Sahadeva and accompanied with the two sons of Kuntī (Bhīma and Arjuna), he left for Indraprastha.

devanagari: गत्वा ते खाण्डवप्रस्थं शङ्खान् दध्मुर्जितारयः । हर्षयन्तः स्वसुहृदो दुर्हृदां चासुखावहाः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: gatvā te khāṇḍavaprasthaṃ śaṅkhān dadhmurjitārayaḥ harṣayantaḥ svasuhṛdo durhṛdāṃ cāsukhāvahāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ ], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ ]
[imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: Having reached Khāṇḍavaprastha, these three warriors who had vanquished their enemies blew their conchs, thrillings their friends with delight and their foes with terror.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा प्रीतमनस इन्द्रप्रस्थनिवासिनः । मेनिरे मागधं शान्तं राजा चाप्तमनोरथः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā prītamanasa indraprasthanivāsinaḥ menire māgadhaṃ śāntaṃ rājā cāptamanorathaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Hearing that sound, the citizens of Indraprastha became delighted in their minds. They concluded that Jarāsandha had been killed and king Yudhiṣṭhira also felt that he had accomplished his object.

devanagari: अभिवन्द्याथ राजानं भीमार्जुनजनार्दनाः । सर्वमाश्रावयां चक्रुः आत्मना यदनुष्ठितम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: abhivandyātha rājānaṃ bhīmārjunajanārdanāḥ sarvamāśrāvayāṃ cakruḥ ātmanā yadanuṣṭhitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Having bowed down to king Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīma, Janārdana and Arjuna reported to him in details all they had severally done.

devanagari: निशम्य धर्मराजस्तत् केशवेनानुकम्पितम् । आनन्दाश्रुकलां मुञ्चन् प्रेम्णा नोवाच किञ्चन ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya dharmarājastat keśavenānukampitam ānandāśrukalāṃ muñcan premṇā novāca kiñcana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muc class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ muñc class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ muñc class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having heard how compassion had been shown by Kṛṣṇa, Dharmarāja shed tears of joy and was too much overwhelmed with love to express a word.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । कृष्णाद्यागमने नाम त्रिसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe kṛṣṇādyāgamane nāma trisaptatitamo
